steps.title: Set up the discriminant condition
steps.content:
For the equation $-x^2 + bx - 49 = 0$ to have no real solutions, the discriminant must be negative:
$$b^2 - 4(-1)(-49) \lt 0$$
$$b^2 - 196 \lt 0$$

steps.title: Solve for $b$
steps.content:
$b^2 \lt 196$ means $-14 \lt b \lt 14$.

The least integer in that range is $b = -13$ (since $b = -14$ gives $b^2 = 196$, not strictly less).

The answer is -13.
